company: ASSA ABLOY AB (Seos / Mobile Keys SDK; IEEE OUI 00:17:7A is registered to ASSA ABLOY)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app com.hidglobal.mobilekeys.android.v3, declared as Java field `LOCK_SERVICE_UUID_BASE`. Classification determined by naming-convention. Phase-4 SDK identification: ASSA ABLOY Seos / Mobile Keys SDK — the lock-credential BLE SDK shipped via com.assaabloy.mobilekeys.api.ble. Observed in three unrelated host apps: com.hidglobal.mobilekeys.android.v3 (HID Global is part of ASSA ABLOY since 2000), com.informationapps.criton.belAirTremblant (Tremblant ski resort hotel keys), sesame.smartspaces.d2i.co (Sesame mobile credentials, ASSA ABLOY Hospitality customer). The UUID base ends in `-00177a000000`, which embeds ASSA ABLOY's IEEE OUI 00:17:7A..

company: Estimote (rotating iBeacon ProximityUUID — anti-spoofing 'SECURE_UUIDS' set)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app com.LLE2023. No Java field-name hint available (likely an obfuscated identifier). Shared-SDK signal: this UUID appeared in many host apps whose package vendors don't share any token, so the original SDK vendor isn't visible from the host-app side. Phase-4 identification (scripts/list_shared_sdk_clusters.py) will upgrade this record. Phase-4 SDK identification: Nordic Semiconductor DFU (Device Firmware Update) service. The 0002a5d5c51b suffix is Nordic's node ID, the d2cc-11e4 middle is the UUIDv1 timestamp at design time. Field names DFU_SERVICE / DFU_PACKET / DFU_CONTROL_POINT / DFU_VERSION confirm the Nordic DFU spec. Distributed inside the Android-DFU-Library and bundled by many Nordic-chip-based BLE products' apps..

company: Estimote Inc. (Estimote Beacon SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app com.LLE2023, declared as Java field `AUTH_SERVICE`. Classification determined by naming-convention. Shared-SDK signal: this UUID appeared in many host apps whose package vendors don't share any token, so the original SDK vendor isn't visible from the host-app side. Phase-4 identification (scripts/list_shared_sdk_clusters.py) will upgrade this record. Phase-4 SDK identification: Estimote Beacon SDK — observed in event/conference apps (com.EventsRCSEngland, com.LLE2023, com.gogoagenda.main.retail) that bundle the Estimote Android SDK for proximity-beacon scanning. UUID family base `-f5f8-466e-aff9-25556b57fe6d` is the canonical Estimote namespace; the first segment encodes (service<<16 | characteristic). Field names captured include ESTIMOTE_SERVICE, EDDYSTONE_UID_NAMESPACE_CHAR, EDDYSTONE_URL_CHAR, OTA_SERVICE, AUTH_SERVICE, CLOUD_AUTH_SERVICE..

company: JNHuaMao Technology (HM-10 BLE module; ffe0+ffe1 also used by many CC2541-based HM-10 clones)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app digifit.android.virtuagym.pro.forgefit24. No Java field-name hint available (likely an obfuscated identifier). Shared-SDK signal: this UUID appeared in many host apps whose package vendors don't share any token, so the original SDK vendor isn't visible from the host-app side. Phase-4 identification (scripts/list_shared_sdk_clusters.py) will upgrade this record. Phase-4 SDK identification: 0xFFE0 service + 0xFFE1 transparent-UART characteristic is the canonical HM-10 BLE module GATT layout. JNHuaMao Technology designed the HM-10 around the TI CC2541; the same UUIDs are reused by clone modules from many vendors (HM-11, AT-09, JDY-08, etc.). Apps that talk to HM-10 or any of its clones carry these..

company: Qingniu Technology Co., Ltd. (Shanghai) — Yolanda smart-scale BLE SDK
UUID_purpose: GATT Characteristic (QINGNIU_WSP_WRITE_CHAR) from Qingniu Scale SDK — WSP scale write characteristic. The Qingniu SDK is shipped by many Chinese smart-scale OEMs (the host APK digifit.android.virtuagym.pro.forgefit24 integrates it for third-party scale support). Phase-4 SDK identification: Qingniu Yolanda smart-scale SDK — common across many fitness apps that resell Chinese-OEM smart scales (CoachCare/Selladore, Virtuagym, Proyecto Featness/Meds, Extrem Racing, WeCo). Uses HM-10/TI-SimpleProfile-style UUID16s (ffe2/ffe3) plus a Qingniu-proprietary abf2 (height-scale notify)..

company: Texas Instruments BLE-Stack SimpleProfile example (widely copied by Chinese CC2541-based BLE module makers)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app branded.com.publicansmanhasset, declared as Java field `aaa065`. Classification determined by usage:getService. Shared-SDK signal: this UUID appeared in many host apps whose package vendors don't share any token, so the original SDK vendor isn't visible from the host-app side. Phase-4 identification (scripts/list_shared_sdk_clusters.py) will upgrade this record. Phase-4 SDK identification: 0xFFA0-0xFFA3 are example service+char UUIDs from TI's BLE-Stack SimpleProfile sample (the demo TI shipped with the CC2540/CC2541 SDK). Hundreds of Chinese BLE module vendors copied these verbatim into shipping products without re-allocating UUIDs. The accompanying example uses them as an Accelerometer Service. The actual host vendor varies per product..

company: Texas Instruments BLE-Stack SimpleProfile example (widely copied by Chinese CC2541-based BLE module makers)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app digifit.android.virtuagym.pro.forgefit24, declared as Java field `f12491a`. Classification determined by naming-convention. Shared-SDK signal: this UUID appeared in many host apps whose package vendors don't share any token, so the original SDK vendor isn't visible from the host-app side. Phase-4 identification (scripts/list_shared_sdk_clusters.py) will upgrade this record. Phase-4 SDK identification: 0xFFF0+0xFFF1 are example service+characteristic UUIDs from TI's BLE-Stack SimpleProfile sample. Copied verbatim by many cheap Chinese BLE modules (alongside the FFAx and FFEx ranges) without proper SIG allocation. The actual host vendor varies..

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7208g`. Classification determined by usage:getService. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7210i`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7211j`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7212k`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7213l`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7214m`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7215n`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7216o`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7217p`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7218q`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Characteristic observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7219r`. Classification determined by usage:getCharacteristic. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.

company: Revolve Robotics (Kubi telepresence robot; shipped via Zoom Android SDK)
UUID_purpose: Custom GATT Service observed in Android app co.diaz.srvol, declared as Java field `f7209h`. Classification determined by usage:getService. This UUID is shipped as part of the Kubi BLE control code inside the Zoom Android SDK (bt.Kubi / bt.KubiManager classes), so it appears in every Android app that embeds Zoom — commonly Indian education / e-learning apps. The host app package id is not the UUID owner; Revolve Robotics is.
